title: cola umbratilis
intro: Cola umbratilis, also known as the Forest cola or Cola gigantea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sterculiaceae. It is native to tropical Africa and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and is traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the bark and roots of the plant are commonly used to make infusions, decoctions, and tonics, which can be consumed to take advantage of the plant's health benefits, including its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain.
